place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Nice and quiet.  Library staff are helpful. But mobile data signal by Simba ( 1 bar inside, 2 -3 bars outside. not sure about the others) is really poor. Couldn't even login to "checking and secure my seat booking", thus the booking got canceled,  and went to someone else.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Queenstown library is a heritage building, rich in its own history. Spacious with two levels, kids area is on the first floor. The second floor for adults is really quiet with nice view over a green park.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
building's exterior may look unassuming or even a lil spooky but the inside is really cozy and homely! lovely space, limited tables but plenty of seats

if you really want a table, they have a table booking system you can use to ensure you have one when you get there!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Nostalgia a plenty in this well preserved library that houses many childhood memories for some.

Located in the quiet side of Queenstown facing sky residences @ Dawson, short walk from Queenstown MRT, a cozy library to spend your time here.

2 levels served by a lift & stairs, lots of reading areas and I particularly enjoy the level 2 quiet reading room facing the greenery.

There are lots of nostalgia and nice touches to this library.

Take your time to explore, read, borrow or exchange. what a privilege!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As of April 2026: It is a old school library with the kinda of nostalgia you get when you visited a library in the 2000's.

Most of the Library's floor space is dedicated to books with working space being rather limited. It's not very convenient to access either as you gotta walk abit from the MRT station and not much amenities nearby.

The tables tend to get booked out until like 7pm ish. With patrons playing musical chairs where someone who has a booking asks those who didn't make a booking to move.

Interesting mix of patrons, with elderly men being the majority and somehow quite a few expats.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This place has been around since we were young. And it is known to host many shops that sells the products for military, police and such.

Lets dive in!

Food:
There have been changes with some maintaining their business around. Most of the time, many would come for tulang or mamak dishes. We kinda like the western food here that is around recently.

Service:
Many shop owners will call you for their business, part of their attention seeking modus operandi. But dont worry bout them, as all seats in this area can be for anyone, thus no dedicated table for any booth.

Atmosphere:
Having been around here for years, we do feel the charm and miss the memories we had while younger here. There have been some works on the place and its way cleaner now compared to the past.

All in all, we definitely enjoy coming over, even for window shopping at the top floors. We applogise there are limited photos of the shops around as we were too hungry actually 🤭🤭

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Besides the ever-popular claypot rice & Hokkien prawn mee (which had a long queue despite the odd hours), try out Dragon Curry’s Nan ru pork cutlet curry rice! Sweet curry with a lemongrass taste, thin pork cutlet but quite tender.

Wen Li Taiwanese’s Pig intestine mee sua set was tasty too, & comes with crispy salt & pepper chicken! For dessert, go for 75 Ah Balling’s Tang yuan with peanut soup / almond milk.

IG @pingguo301

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There are actually three levels at Golden Mile Food Centre. Most will visit the 1st and 2nd level where many good and popular food stalls are located but little people visit the 3rd level where shops selling military merchandise are located. Do pay a visit while you’re there to see the many interesting merchandises on display.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Not able to fairly gage the food here because it has lots of food stalls I'd only tried the glutinous ball peanut soup that tasted just like my mom's home made peanut soup yum2. Generous portion of peanuts! And 6 glutinous balls or tang yuan made of 5 different flavors you can choose from at SGD3.50 wow! I ate 2 bowls and I came back again for another 2 bowls! I brought company hahaha~ 😋😋

A Sarawak friend i brought thinks the auntie made the 5 flavor glutinous balls herself because the thickness of the tangyuan were not even or same with other dessert stalls.

Personally I still love peanut and sesame flavors best. But I'm pleasantly surprised by green tea or matcha flavor. My friends love the yam flavor. I like red bean paste too but peanut and sesame are sweeter and crunchier with something to bite I needed more of those 2 flavors or I'll get a 3rd bowl. 🤣🤣

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This Halal stall next to Ashes Burnnit, fried delicious Kway Teow Goreng. The noodle is abit moist if anyone like it fried this way. The prawn is cut in smaller size & fresh too. Portion is good for heavy lunch at S$4.50, however is a little chilly, so prepare a cold drink in hand.
I had try their Laska Johor before which is creamy & different from SG laksa.
Stall mainly served seafood dishes, No beef & mutton.

Jan’26, revisited the stall and managed to photo the stall name. Ordered the Mee Goreng also Kway Teow Goreng and she take time to fried it. The taste remain same as last eaten but this time less spicy. The Mee Goreng is slightly on a wet/moist dish that differs from traditional Indian mee goreng with potato.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
For anyone who's experienced the thrill of a National Stadium event – be it an electrifying concert or a nail-biting sports match – Stadium MRT Station (CC6) is your gateway to the events held at the Singapore National Stadium.

Located within minutes walk from the stadium, convenience is just the beginning. This station consistently stands out for its well maintenance, cleanliness, spacious, and excellent ventilation, ensuring a comfortable experience even on the busiest event nights. What truly sets Stadium MRT apart is its unwavering efficiency. You'll always find additional staff on hand, guiding foot traffic and managing crowds with a seamless flow. They even go the extra mile, posting last train schedules upon your arrival, so you can plan your exit with peace of mind.

And then there's the "mini after-party" – a unique and brilliant crowd control strategy implemented after concerts. It transforms what could be a chaotic rush into a surprisingly organized and even enjoyable experience. BIG KUDOS to the station management team for the commitment to a smooth and pleasant journey for every event-goer.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Stadium MRT Station is a modern and well-designed station that serves as a key gateway to Singapore Sports Hub. The station is clean, spacious, and clearly signposted, making navigation easy even during large events or peak hours. Its wide platforms and multiple exits help manage crowds efficiently, especially on concert or match days. Accessibility is thoughtfully planned, with lifts, escalators, and barrier-free access for wheelchair users and families with strollers. The station’s sleek architecture and open feel add to a pleasant commuting experience. Connectivity is convenient, with sheltered walkways leading to nearby venues, parks, and waterfront areas. While it can get crowded during major events, train frequency is usually increased to cope with demand. Overall, Stadium MRT Station is reliable, efficient, and well integrated into its surroundings, making it a comfortable and practical stop for both daily commuters and event-goers.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Just want to say a shoutout to our SMRT staff at stadium, Hisham who assisted me today, around 7.25 to 7.30pm. Really appreciate his kindness and willingness to help. Kudos to him!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Excellent, love at first sight, the very first impression of taking escalators down the train boarding area was a feeling of going down to a swimming pool themed of it’s unique green coloured floor ceramic tiles, just love this vibes, the place was discreetly cleaned and neat, it’s my first time dropping by here , the connectivity to Kallang leisure Park was only stone throw never seen such convenient and well planned and thought of, link between a shopping mall with covered walkways, u have done it so well SMRT, kudos to you guys.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: One of my favourite MRT stations in Singapore! Always really clean and well maintained, which makes for a great first impression especially for foreigners visiting events around the Sports Hub or Indoor Stadium. Also sheltered walkway to the Kallang Wave Mall and Kallang Leisurepark. Always plenty of helpful staff around, especially for big events like the ongoing Taylor Swift concert so people know where to go. I love that their staff also make sure commuters on wheelchairs can board the train safely.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I want to send my appreciation to the MRT staffs on duty today. They were very helpful and supportive when an incident happened at 230pm and took action immediately to help. Really thankful to have people with such big hearts

review_text: Small but complete and convenient mrt station. Directly connected to some buses routes. Shops, restaurants, low cost accommodation, parks just across the street. Some restaurant are included directly at the mrt station. Clean and comfortable. Clear signage.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Very good MRT station. Not available at other stations but this one has the value checker and also the cute robot but it was resting 💤💤💤

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Very clean. Good, they give some space to seat while you waiting. They have many informantion boards with 3 languages, so useful for foreigners. There is a timer for the up coming MRT too..

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Kallang MRT (EW10) is one of those "old but gold" stations. Not as fancy as the new Downtown Line or Thomson-East Coast Line stations, but very functional and steady.

​The Good Things:
​Convenience: It's an elevated station, so the breeze is quite shiok when waiting for train. If you going to Stadium for concert or football, this is the "secret" alternative. Got one long sheltered walkway all the way to Sports Hub, no need to worry about rain or sun.

​Food & Amenities: Outside the station got many things. If hungry, just walk a bit to Geylang Lorong 1—plenty of good food like the famous beef kway teow or frog porridge. Inside the station also got Mr. Bean and Old Chang Kee for quick bite.

​Accessibility: Right next to the Lorong 1 Geylang Bus Terminal. Very easy to switch from MRT to bus if you heading to Upper Boon Keng or Geylang area.

​The "Can Improve" Part:
​Crowd: Wah, if got Jay Chou or Taylor Swift concert, please be prepared. The station will be super packed. Even though got the bridge, the queue to enter the gantries can be quite scary.
​Vibe: The station looks a bit "uncle" already lah, standard 80s/90s design. Everything clean but just looks slightly dated compared to the newer lines.

​Verdict: Overall, a very reliable station. Clean, well-maintained, and connects you to some of the best food spots in SG. Just avoid the peak hour "concert crowd" can already.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC)
Review on 20/1/2026

The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC), founded in 1883 and set along the iconic Padang, is one of Singapore’s most historic social‑sports institutions. Its clubhouse offers a wide range of sports, recreation, dining, and event facilities that continue to serve a vibrant and diverse community.

The club’s most striking feature is its grand entrance, framed by two sweeping curved staircases. This graceful, ceremonial design creates a sense of ascending into a place of heritage and dignity. Against the backdrop of the Padang and the Civic District, the entrance stands out as a timeless and memorable architectural landmark.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Private club in central district with roofed warm and cold swimming pool, restaurant, bar with live band and cafe.
Cost environment for private gathering and easily accessible by MRT via City Hall station

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Dinner with an old friend at the SRC'S Chinese Restaurant. Reminiscent of the old days in Singapore, with traditions alive and well. Very pleasant evening 😊😋

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Our family had a wonderful visit to the Singapore Recreation Club (SRC) during their open house! The location right by the Padang is fantastic, and we were impressed that the facilities are truly top-notch. The pool looked inviting, and the entire club was clean and prestigious. It's clear why it's a desirable spot, but be aware that it is indeed for members only. We really enjoyed our temporary visit and appreciated seeing the high quality of the club.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Not a member but, their facility is quite complete. From dance, restaurant, chill drinking club, outside chill area, car parking, swimming pool, gym, to pickleball court

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: One of the best national libraries I’ve seen, with a large number of books to rent. 24/7 book drop, a lot of magazines available to read, plenty of quiet, comfy and beautiful areas to read in, and that’s all just the basement level – there are 15 floors all with their own content. place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Perhaps the largest public library in Singapore. Hard to find a librarian for assistance, and bookings for seats are required if you do not want to be asked to leave just because someone has booked the seats. A really stupid concept for those who would casually come into the library if they are in the area. Throughout the years, NLB has done nothing to enhance the library experience. What the public has been slapped with are rules and regulations which makes visiting the library a chore.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Came purely for the kids section of the library and it sure lived up to our expectations. The little one thoroughly enjoyed himself amidst the aquarium themed library and the endless literature around the area. Very thoughtfully, they also offer floor cushions where you can take and just find a spot to read and chill out on. The hit area with the deck chairs is the central of the kids section but was unfortunately pretty much always filled with people. Great place to spend the weekend letting the kids immerse themselves. place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The Central Public Library in Singapore is a vibrant space blending innovation, design, and functionality. Located at the National Library Building, it offers an extensive collection of books, digital resources, and themed spaces catering to all age groups. Highlights include the eco-friendly My Tree House, the world’s first green library for children, and its lush ambiance. The library’s modern facilities, cozy reading nooks, and seamless technology make it an ideal place for study or leisure. Regular workshops and events add to its community spirit. A hub of knowledge and creativity, it’s a must-visit for book lovers and lifelong learners.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Today, I attended Emma Memma's Performance at Drama Centre. I grew up watching Sesame Street and Hi-5, and of course, The Wiggles. So when I learned that Emma Memma, formerly of The Wiggles, was performing, I was really excited.

What I find especially meaningful is the way Emma Memma and Elvin Melvin connects through sign language. For that, kudos to them.

Some children can only sing and communicate through sign language — yet they’re often overlooked in theatre shows. That’s why Emma Memma’s performances stand out: she reminds us that inclusion matters, and every child deserves to shine. (Catch her in action on youtube.)

Drop by our libraries or check out the NLB online portal and mobile app! You’ll find a huge variety of e-books, e-magazines, audiobooks, and lots of other exciting features. Truly one of the best resources you can enjoy in Singapore 🙂

Have you seen the sky-bridged library, on the upper floors, rising 16 storeys high? Designed with floor-to-ceiling glass panels, it welcomes natural light into every corner, creating a warm, open atmosphere. Look out for the greenery that softens the sleek architecture, blending nature with structure to offer a calming, inviting space.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Leisure Park Kallang is a laid-back shopping center with just the right amount of traffic—not too crowded, making it comfortable to explore.
It offers a bit of everything: a kids’ arcade, tuition centers, a bowling alley, a food court, and numerous restaurants and cafés.
Overall, it’s a good place to visit if you prefer a quieter, more relaxed atmosphere away from the usual mall crowds.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This mall, especially when viewed against the backdrop of its more prominent neighbors, is relatively underwhelming.

While it boasts a good selection of restaurants and food options, the overall shopping experience feels somewhat haphazard in my opinion. It's certainly not the first mall that comes to mind when you're looking to do some serious shopping.

Given that the primary patrons of the Kallang/Stadium area are typically athletes, sports enthusiasts, or concert-goers, I can only envision them visiting this mall predominantly for its food offerings.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Very near MRT, the shops here always opened and closed forever more like pop up stores as business has been bad with weak traffic after the open air carpark has closed for the temp swimming competition but when it is over since late 2025, the structure seems to be left there rotting. The level 1 food court is nice, very big supermarket at B1 Sheng Song. Open and shelter carpark lots available. Many eateries with Burger King as the only fast food and a KTV outlet beside it. Level 2 is very boring apart from a bowling allery. Nothing much for tourists to shop or see.

Walking distance to Kallang Wave Mall.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Awesome supermarket Sheng Siong experience! 😊 Rarely do I rave about supermarkets, but this one nailed it. Went on a Friday late morning - no crowds, spotless, HUGE, and super well-stocked. Layout's beautiful, service is on point (just like any other SS), and parking's a breeze. Can't ask for more, what a gem!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Quite an underwhelming & empty shopping mall located near Stadium MRT. This mall has many empty shops, questionable empty spaces, as well as rooftop which is only accessible to the management office workers. I see a need for this mall to touch up on its space management and administration to make it more vibrant and increase the number of visitors

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly an amazing place and definitely a must-visit attraction in Singapore. The entire area is beautifully designed, extremely clean, and very well maintained, making it a wonderful experience for visitors of all ages.

The Flower Dome and Cloud Forest offer a unique opportunity to see plants and flowers from different parts of the world. During our visit, we also experienced the Tulip Feast, where we saw many beautiful varieties of tulips in full bloom. The colorful display was absolutely stunning and added a vibrant touch to the overall experience. The indoor waterfall inside the Cloud Forest is especially impressive and creates a refreshing atmosphere. The Supertree Grove is another major highlight, and the night light show there feels absolutely magical with the music and colorful lights.

The whole place is spacious and easy to explore, although there is a lot to walk around, so it can feel a little tiring after some time. Still, the beautiful views and attractions make the walking completely worth it.

There are many perfect photo spots throughout the gardens, especially during the evening when the night view looks stunning. Souvenir shops are also available if you would like to buy gifts or memories from your visit.

Overall, it’s a peaceful and beautiful place to relax, enjoy nature, and experience one of Singapore’s most iconic attractions. Highly recommended, especially for first-time visitors.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly a highlight of Singapore and absolutely not to be missed. I’d highly recommend getting a combo ticket for the Cloud Forest and Flower Dome—plan for about 2-3 hours to cover both.

The Cloud Forest is spectacular, almost Jurassic Park-like in parts, and enjoyable for both kids and adults. There’s misting at specific times, so it’s worth checking those in advance. A great way to explore is to head to the top level first and then make your way down, stopping at the different exhibits along the way.
The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, showcasing flowers and plants from around the world, with plenty of photo-worthy corners throughout. There’s also a nice gift shop with garden and flower-themed souvenirs.

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
During my recent visit to Singapore, one of the most memorable places I explored was Gardens by the Bay. This remarkable attraction is a perfect example of how nature, technology, and sustainability can come together to create something truly extraordinary.

From the moment I entered the gardens, I was impressed by the sheer scale and beauty of the place. The landscape is meticulously maintained, featuring a stunning variety of plants, flowers, and themed gardens from around the world. The iconic Supertree Grove is undoubtedly the highlight, with its futuristic structures creating a breathtaking skyline, especially during the evening when they are beautifully illuminated.

I also had the opportunity to visit the conservatories, where the climate-controlled environments showcase unique plant species from different regions. Walking through these attractions felt like traveling across multiple ecosystems in a single day. The attention to detail, cleanliness, and visitor-friendly facilities were outstanding.

What impressed me most was Singapore’s commitment to environmental sustainability, which is reflected throughout the entire garden. Despite being located in the heart of a modern city, the gardens provide a peaceful escape where visitors can relax, take photographs, and appreciate nature.

Whether you are traveling with family, friends, or exploring solo, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit destination. It offers a unique blend of natural beauty, innovation, and world-class design that leaves a lasting impression. My visit was truly unforgettable, and I would highly recommend it to anyone planning a trip to Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

Beyond the conservatories, there is so much to discover. The Supertrees are iconic, the outdoor gardens are peaceful and surprisingly diverse, and even simple walks around the lakes reveal interesting plants, wildlife, and photo opportunities. I particularly enjoyed wandering through areas that many visitors overlook, including the lily-covered ponds and the themed garden displays

Another pleasant surprise was discovering that the Conservatory Gift Shop leads into a complimentary Jurassic World Experience, which added an unexpected element of fun to the visit. Combined with convenient access via MRT, dining options such as Satay by the Bay, excellent facilities, and world-class landscaping, it is easy to spend an entire day here without getting bored

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
During my recent visit to Singapore, one of the most memorable places I explored was Gardens by the Bay. This remarkable attraction is a perfect example of how nature, technology, and sustainability can come together to create something truly extraordinary.

From the moment I entered the gardens, I was impressed by the sheer scale and beauty of the place. The landscape is meticulously maintained, featuring a stunning variety of plants, flowers, and themed gardens from around the world. The iconic Supertree Grove is undoubtedly the highlight, with its futuristic structures creating a breathtaking skyline, especially during the evening when they are beautifully illuminated.

I also had the opportunity to visit the conservatories, where the climate-controlled environments showcase unique plant species from different regions. Walking through these attractions felt like traveling across multiple ecosystems in a single day. The attention to detail, cleanliness, and visitor-friendly facilities were outstanding.

What impressed me most was Singapore’s commitment to environmental sustainability, which is reflected throughout the entire garden. Despite being located in the heart of a modern city, the gardens provide a peaceful escape where visitors can relax, take photographs, and appreciate nature.

Whether you are traveling with family, friends, or exploring solo, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit destination. It offers a unique blend of natural beauty, innovation, and world-class design that leaves a lasting impression. My visit was truly unforgettable, and I would highly recommend it to anyone planning a trip to Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

Beyond the conservatories, there is so much to discover. The Supertrees are iconic, the outdoor gardens are peaceful and surprisingly diverse, and even simple walks around the lakes reveal interesting plants, wildlife, and photo opportunities. I particularly enjoyed wandering through areas that many visitors overlook, including the lily-covered ponds and the themed garden displays

Another pleasant surprise was discovering that the Conservatory Gift Shop leads into a complimentary Jurassic World Experience, which added an unexpected element of fun to the visit. Combined with convenient access via MRT, dining options such as Satay by the Bay, excellent facilities, and world-class landscaping, it is easy to spend an entire day here without getting bored

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Tucked within the vibrant heart of Tekka Food Centre, 蒸好味 Steamed Fish Cuisine is a quiet ode to the beauty of honest, well-executed cooking.

What sets this humble gem apart is its unwavering commitment to freshness — each fish is live-caught for the day, delivering a delicate sweetness that simply cannot be replicated. Gently steamed to perfection, the flesh remains tender and succulent, elevated only by a refined touch of ginger and scallion that enhances rather than overpowers. Every bite speaks of clarity, balance, and respect for the ingredient.

Equally noteworthy is their steamed egg — a silky, comforting masterpiece crafted from real eggs cracked fresh on the spot, not pre-mixed liquid substitutes. The result is a custard-like texture that is impossibly smooth, rich, and deeply satisfying.

In a setting as bustling as Tekka, this stall stands out not through extravagance, but through precision, sincerity, and an unwavering dedication to quality. A true hidden indulgence for those who appreciate the quiet luxury of simplicity done exceptionally well.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The food never disappoints—every bite hits the spot. While the service is decent, there’s still room to elevate the overall experience. If you want to enjoy it at its best, come before lunchtime… unless you don’t mind a long, hungry wait in line.
And once you’re seated, don’t hold back—order the duck webs, pig ears, and duck wings. Paired with an ice-cold beer, it’s the kind of combo that keeps you reaching for more.
One visit is all it takes—you’ll already be planning your next.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The first time I tried the food stalls here was on a Sunday afternoon. It was crowded and I got overwhelmed. I came back on a Wednesday and had tremendous service even though we barely spoke the same language.  Food was delicious. I found a clean spot to sit and eat and soak in the atmosphere.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Actually went there to try again the famous 545 whampoa prawn noodle. Saw this stall (several stall from 545). An old amah alone selling. Surprisingnya the food taste good. And worth the price for a bowl like this.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Did a market walk at Tekka Centre — a true slice of Singapore life! 🇸🇬

First built in 1915 as Kandang Kerbau Market, this Little India landmark has evolved into a vibrant multi-use hub:

B1 – convenient parking below

Will be back to explore more of Level 2 soon!😻

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Even though this library is not as big as Woodlands and Jurong East Regional Libraries, it still carries plenty of books and it’s spacious enough too with plenty of comfortable seats.
I also like the more relaxed vibe here with plenty of plants.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

I wish that the Geylang East Library was closer to where I stayed so I could go there more often. The library has a nice location because it’s just 5 minutes away from the MRT. The library has 2 levels. On the first level there is the borrowing books section, and right across the hall there is a children books section. The children’s section is one of the best children’s sections I’ve seen. When I had walked in I noticed immediately that the room was not tiny. The children’s section is a good size for children because some children are very hyper, so if they want to find books that they are interested in they can just keep walking around because the room is a very nice size, and find books. The second level has Cooking, Singapore Collection, Fiction, Non-fiction, Business, and YP (Young People) books. I like it when libraries have books on display so I can maybe browse through them and that’s what the Geylang library had. There’s also this nice long sofa that multiple people can sit on and also a nice place to use a computer on. There are also a few seats and smaller tables in the middle close to the sofa and that’s really nice for having a pile of books and just reading for the day. Overall, I think the Geylang East library is a very nice place to study, use your computer, and read for the whole day if you’re up for it.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Air quality in this library is really bad. I don’t think it’s suitable for kids/teens. Carpet floor collects so much dust, should be replaced. This is my go to library in my pri school days, but now I come out of it having a sore throat due to a sensitive nose

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The library has a dedicated children's section that is welcoming, engaging and safe. The kid's area is designed with bright colors, comfy seating and fun decorations.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: A pure reading encounter, with no shopping centres to distract kids.  Kids are calm and people are not anxious.  Such a pity it’s going to get demolished soon.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Staff is just ignoring you. Asked for a menu at the bar two times - the lady said “give me a minute” and never came back. And it’s not because they’re super busy - they just don’t care. This is simply disrespectful

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Restaurant have several hit and miss cuisines but the most recent visit had me trying the fish and fries. It was really good and affordable. The fish was not overly battered and there were thick flakes of fish within that is light and fluffy!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Singapore Swimming Club has a great selection of Indian food and have always enjoyed the food, especially someone who is very discerning. Having said that, off late the quality of paneer dishes seems to be off. The paneer tikka is no longer soft, creamy and flavourful. I think they may have changed their supplier or switched to using frozen paneer. The management should look into it and bring back the original quality paneer.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Been coming here for years since I’ve been a kid. Always love how homely and family-friendly this place is. The eateries are fantastic, especially the cafe and Chinese restaurant. The club sports facilities are excellent, I’ve used the pool, tennis courts, bowling alley, and gym before. With the Katong Park station almost ready nearby, it will become even more accessible.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Went there for the second time as my aunt is their member. The first round tried their lunch buffet. Decent taste in many varieties. This round tried their fried hokkien mee. Having said that, off late the quality of paneer dishes seems to be off. The paneer tikka is no longer soft, creamy and flavourful. I think they may have changed their supplier or switched to using frozen paneer. The management should look into it and bring back the original quality paneer.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Been coming here for years since I’ve been a kid. Always love how homely and family-friendly this place is. The eateries are fantastic, especially the cafe and Chinese restaurant. The club sports facilities are excellent, I’ve used the pool, tennis courts, bowling alley, and gym before. With the Katong Park station almost ready nearby, it will become even more accessible.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Went there for the second time as my aunt is their member. The first round tried their lunch buffet. Decent taste in many varieties. This round tried their fried hokkien mee. The taste was perfect, the portion was big. But wait for my local coffee for a long time. This really needs to improve

review_text: Awesome running track with canopy! You can go easy or treshold, anything you like to go that day! No worry of raining. The colorful track also boost the feeling. Although a lil bit “effort” to go here, but it is worth it!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
We attend the Monster Jam 2025, Epic trucks, but Pit Party value gap — no freebies for kids.

We paid extra for Pit Party access and walked into… a merch zone. No free keepsakes at all—just items for sale and a small colouring table. For a premium add-on aimed at families, that’s a miss.

What the Pit Party should include (at minimum):
•	Free autograph cards or mini posters for each featured truck
•	Sticker sheet or Pit Party “passport” kids can stamp at each truck
•	Lanyard/badge (souvenir access pass)
•	Foam earplugs for children (practical + appreciated)

If nothing physical is possible, at least offer a QR code for printable posters/cards and a digital pit scavenger hunt with a small prize pickup.

There’s also a two-hour dead zone from pit close (4:00 pm) to show start (6:00 pm). Schedule some pre-show content—driver Q&A, trivia on the big screen, or early-seat entertainment—so families aren’t just waiting.

On-track: the stunts were fantastic.

Audio: the PA was muddy and unintelligible for interviews/commentary—in the pit, at our seats, and around the concourse. Needs proper EQ and coverage.

Safety: our exit staircase had two failed lights. One staff member with a torch stood by the second; the first was unlit and unattended. Please fix lighting and staff both points.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Great place! Visited OCBC Arena for Singapore Smash 2026. The staff was reliable and helpful, especially Shafiq from the gym who helped me understand the gym and opening hours! 谢谢

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Best place to be at in Singapore for sporting events. State of the art facilities and good crowd control throughout.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Good functional sports facilities hub and stadium. Transportation is terrific with the MRT station next to the stadium and also bus stops nearby. The stadium was well-constructed for large scale events and the effects were spectacular. Unfortunately it was raining when I was attending a concert. Instead of using the sheltered walkway, due to poor planning and organization of the organizer, the spectators were made to queue in the rain for bag check. This resulted in unnecessary hassle and unpleasantness of the experience 😒

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
This MRT theme is Old Singapore art paintings.

Take time to observe, we could see four to five old Singapore paintings showcased.

The one at  train level is the big art showing Sim Lim towers and old Auro Rickshaws. These are very heart touching and remembering our old Singapore life style.

This MRT has a small Backery and Cloth Store as well inside.

One thing must to say, we need to go down 5 levels to catch train means this is one of the deepest MRT in City.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: The ramp / walkway leading up to the escalator is so dirty and looked like it have never been clean before with so many stains left for months or even longer than that. Upkeep of cleanliness here is 1 of the worst among most MRT stations. Passenger service is one of the worst here.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Singapore's MTR and transportation system is not a question at all. The train were quite new and well maintained, clean inside and fewer passengers. The MTR system map is also accessible online. Jalan Besar station were near cheaper hotels and asian cuisine. Please follow my fb at Cybercode Shan and Shan Cabillan on Tktok for more travel updates!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Beautiful clean environment.
There's a shop called Stella, Super comfy leggings and palazzo pants. Bought few 3/4 leggings and nice tank top. Material is super cooling and best for this Sg weather.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Nice very calm place.
We can buy jewels, cloths, fruits and many more there.
It is nearby little India and Serangoon Rd, you can vist temples and more fun over there.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Old Airport Road Food Centre exceeded my expectations with a great variety of Singapore heritage dishes. I also loved the creativity behind many stall names referencing places like Chong Pang, Kallang, and Blanco Court — adds local charm.

• Old Airport Road Fried Hokkien Mee (#01-99) – 4/5: Wet-style Hokkien Mee with decent wok hei, generous prawns, good value.

• Famous Old Airport Fried Oyster (#01-101) – 3/5: Soggy omelette, overcooked prawns, not worth $10.

• Chong Pang Huat BBQ (#01-90) – 3/5: Expensive, small portions, hygiene issues.

• Jin Fried Rice (#01-63) – 5/5: Fragrant mala fried rice with pork chop — excellent, must-try.

• Chuan Loo Bee Hokkien Food (#01-111) – 2/5: Tough pork ribs, odd sauce, disappointing.

• Whampoa Soya Bean (#01-68) – 4/5: Solid traditional desserts; soya drinks slightly diluted.

• Unkai Japanese Cuisine (#01-88) – 5/5: Generous salmon bowl, wallet-friendly, satisfying.

• Western Barbeque (#01-53) – 3/5: Chicken chop okay but lacks flavour; queues are long.

• Famous Kallang Airport Fried Oyster (#01-55) – 2/5: Oily, overwhelming, still hunting for a better version.

• Salt (#01-128) – 4/5: Aglio Olio with teriyaki chicken — decent but slightly pricey.

• Yi Ji Fried Prawn Noodles (#01-73) – 3.5/5: Carrot cake decent, noodles not outstanding.

• Xin Mei Xiang Lor Mee (#01-116) – 4/5: Double Fish Lor Mee decent; broth mediocre, some service issues.

• Heritage Tanglin Puff – 5/5: Singapore institution since 1952. Freshly fried puffs with generous fillings — curry potato and curry chicken are standouts. 6 for $12, great value.

• Ah Yee Hong Kong Roasted (#01-77) – 4/5: Three-meat combo rice ($10) — char siew well caramelised, roast pork had satisfying crackling, but roast duck was chewy and rice was dry. Chilli a nice touch. Would love a chicken option on the menu. Worth a visit for the char siew and roast pork.

Overall, a must-visit for variety and heritage flavours, though some stalls are hit-or-miss. Will keep exploring and updating!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Old Airport Road Food Center is one of popular places for breakfast and lunch.  There are many nice food around such as Lor Mee, Hokkien Mee, Prawn Noddle, Chicken Rice, Roasted Pork and Duck, Western Food, Wanton Noddle.  There is also a new NTUC at level 2.  This food Center can be quite packed during peaked hours.  If you are around Kallang area, I will recommend you guys to drop by …

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Great hawker centre but it can get a little stuffy. Try to grab one of the round tables by the entrances or near the ventilation areas for more space and cool air.

Loved the lor mee! Char koay teow and prawn mee we’re decent too.

If you’re getting ais kacang or cendol, get it from the popular stall in the middle to avoid disappointment!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Very nice and patient uncle who runs this stall. I always thought sugar canes all tasted the same, until I tried this. Happened to find a seat in front of this stall and decided to just buy from him. The sugar cane was really fresh and had a clean taste (there was not that sugary aftertaste). Uncle was also telling me that his is not soaked in sugar syrup and yet it was still naturally sweet! Highly recommended!

I think $2 for a cup, extra for lemon.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: One of my favourite food centres. Great selection and food. Easy to get to via Bus 36 from Changi. Due for a nice power cleaning though.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

review_text:
Easiest way to take bus to Woodlands checkpoint using Causeway link cross border bus. Only SGD 4.80.

There is also sbs transit and SJE bus operating from this terminal

Accessibility: 400 meter to Bugis MRT (EWL/Green Line/ DWL Blue Line) and also can access to Jalan Besar MRT (DWL/ Blue Line)

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: To get to JB from here is easy. If you want to sit in the bus, line up on the right side but if you don’t kind standing, line up on the left side.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Why is there nowhere to sit?
Now some days later after arriving at Kuala Lumpur’s beautiful bus terminal, the comparison to Singapore’s terminal is extreme. KL’s is like a modern airport terminal, Singapore’s Queen Street is more like a empty boot sale

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Overall, the transportation itself was smooth and we had no issues with the ride. However, our experience at the station was quite disappointing due to the attitude of one of the staff members.
We were traveling with a young child and accidentally walked into the station area because we did not notice the signage, which was quite small and easy to miss. Instead of guiding us in a calm and helpful manner, an elderly staff member raised his voice at us in a way that felt unnecessary and unfriendly.
We completely understand that there are rules to follow, and we had no intention of going the wrong way. A polite explanation would have been much more appropriate, especially considering we were with a child.
We hope the staff can be reminded to communicate more kindly with visitors. A little patience and understanding can make a big difference in creating a welcoming environment for everyone.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The terminal itself is easy to find, but my experience with the security staff was very poor. While crossing a zebra crossing with no buses entering or exiting, a guard revved his engine at me. He then aggressively asked if I could speak English and if I was "blind," pointing to a sign. When I pointed out it was a zebra crossing, he shouted that the rule was only for bus drivers.

According to Singapore's Highway Code, pedestrians have the right of way on pedestrian crossings. The guard's aggressive and incorrect interpretation of the rules makes pedestrians feel unsafe. Management urgently needs to retrain its security team on basic traffic rules and respectful communication.
